transport.operation: POST {base}/graphql — queries and mutations always POST; GET exists only for introspection

headers.authorization: bearer <token> — the GraphQL docs use the lowercase 'bearer' scheme

transport.body: {"query": <string>, "variables": <object>} — values are passed through the separate variables object, never string-interpolated into the query text

inventory_query.shape: organization(login: $login) { repositories(first: $first, after: $after) { totalCount pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } nodes { id name nameWithOwner isArchived visibility pushedAt } } } plus a top-level rateLimit { limit cost remaining used resetAt } selection
inventory_query.page_size: first/last accept 1-100; the inventory defaults to 100 and clamps anything larger
inventory_query.first_page: after is null or omitted on the first request

pagination.traversal: pass each response's pageInfo.endCursor verbatim as the next request's after variable; stop when hasNextPage is false
pagination.retry: a 502/503 page fetch is retried exactly once with the identical cursor; a second consecutive failure propagates as the typed HTTP error
pagination.dedup: repositories are deduplicated by node id across pages (a retried page can overlap); first-seen order is kept and the dropped count reported

rate_limit.field: rateLimit { limit cost remaining used resetAt } — point-based primary limit; the report carries the LAST page's values
rate_limit.headers: x-ratelimit-limit / -remaining / -used are integers, x-ratelimit-reset is UTC epoch seconds, x-ratelimit-resource is 'graphql'

errors.partial: a 200 response may carry BOTH data and errors; keep the non-null nodes, drop null nodes, and preserve each error's message/type/path as warnings
errors.fatal: errors with no usable data (organization null) raise a typed GraphQL error preserving the errors array
errors.http: non-2xx raises a typed error with status and the response message
errors.redaction: the token and the authorization header value never appear in error text

test_fixture_notes.pages: p1: mars-rover-fw + ops-runbooks (endCursor CUR1); p2: quality-gate (CUR2); p3: paint-batch-api (hasNextPage false); retry scenario replays p2 with an ops-runbooks overlap
